Caption: Graphical model of Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models.
Graphical model for non-Markovian inference models.

The FoilDiff architecture, incorporating an encoder, a latent transformer, and a decoder, with U-Net-style skip connections and deep latent embeddings to integrate conditional information effectively.

Latent transformer architectures. (a) Standard DiT architecture illustrating the conventional diffusion transformer pipeline [30]; (b) U-ViT architecture showing modifications [50]. Z ∈ R B × M denotes the latent representation obtained at the final block of the encoder, where B is the batch size, M the embedding dimension, and D is the total number of blocks.

Comparison of predicted and reference flow fields for representative test cases. The left side displays the ground-truth CFD flow fields, and the right FoilDiff’s reconstructed pressure and velocity fields.

Flow predictions for a single test case (Case 7). The predicted pressure and velocity fields for six individual samples are shown by the six columns with the ellipsis indicating omitted intermediate samples. Caption: Logarithmic plot of mean squared error (MSE) of the recommended-sized Aifnet model compared with FoilDiff variants after 150k training iterations.

Surface flow quantities predicted by FoilDiff and a DiT-based baseline against the CFD ground truth for E598 airfoil at Re = 6.482 × 10 6 at an angle of − 2 ∘ .
